item.title: US healthcare marketplaces shared citizenship and race data with ad tech giants
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Virginia and Washington D.C. paused the data collection and sharing, after Bloomberg's investigation found their health insurance marketplaces were sharing users' information with advertisers.

item.title: Amazon opens up its global logistics network to all businesses
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The new service, called Amazon Supply Chain Services, pits the e-commerce giant directly against UPS and FedEx.

item.title: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The BOGO offer is live. For a limited time, buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. Offer ends this Friday, May 8. Save here.

item.title: Barocal can cool your food and drink by squeezing a hunk of plastic crystals
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Barocal might have discovered a cheap, nonpolluting material that could dethrone today's refrigeration technology.

item.title: Doordash adds AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding, edit photos of dishes
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: DoorDash on Monday added new AI-powered tools that let merchants speed up onboarding, edit photos to make dishes look better, and create new websites from existing content.

item.title: Ouster’s new color lidar is coming to replace cameras
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: A sensor that can simultaneously capture depth and image data has long been a "holy grail," Ouster CEO Angus Pacala told TechCrunch.

item.title: Nicolas Sauvage is betting on the boring parts of AI
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The portfolio he has assembled since 2019 is dotted with technologies that have become more widely interesting to VCs over the last year:

item.title: We’ll take it: a TikToker rallies pledges to buy Spirit Airlines after its abrupt weekend collapse
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Within hours he'd thrown up a website — a janky, one-hour job, by his own admission — and by Sunday, 36,000 "founding patrons" had pledged nearly $23 million, crashing his servers in the process.

item.title: ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The ad comes from Artisan, the AI startup behind billboards urging businesses to "stop hiring humans."

item.title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: A new study examines how large language models perform in a variety of medical contexts, including real emergency room cases — where at least one model seemed to be more accurate than human doctors.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: How do you issue a ticket to a robotaxi?
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

item.title: This tiny, magnetic e-reader could stop you from doomscrolling
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The Xteink X3 is a delightfully tiny, MagSafe-compatible e-ink reader that attaches to the back of your phone like a Pop Socket.

item.title: AI-generated actors and scripts are now ineligible for Oscars
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.category: academy of motion picture arts and sciences
item.description: Bad news for Tilly Norwood.

item.title: Farewell, Jeeves: Ask.com shuts down
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Owner IAC says it's discontinuing its search business.

item.title: Netflix delays Greta Gerwig’s ‘Narnia’ movie for big theatrical push in 2027
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "The Magician's Nephew" looks like a big next step in Netflix's thawing relationship with movie theaters.

item.title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: AI-powered dictation apps are useful for replying to emails, taking notes, and even coding through your voice

item.title: Beyond Lovable and Mistral: 21 European startups to watch
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: It is not that European startups never get attention — Lovable and Mistral AI are proof of that. But there are many more that insiders are tracking.

item.title: Uber wants to turn its millions of drivers into a sensor grid for self-driving companies
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Praveen Neppalli Naga, Uber's chief technology officer, revealed the plan in an interview at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, describing it as a natural extension of a nascent program the company announced in late January called AV Labs.

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now: in a world where rival Cursor is reportedly in talks to be acquired by SpaceX for $60 billion, is Replit also bound to sell?

item.title: Musely secures $360M from General Catalyst without giving up equity
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The DTC skin, hair, and menopause care brand will use the non-dilutive capital to super-charge customer acquisition.
